_doc: STANDARD CIRCUIT BLOCKS (电路块库) — the topology tier above standard-parts.json.
A block is a KNOWN-GOOD peripheral subcircuit (CH340 USB-serial, ESP32 auto-download,
ESP32-S3 module, button, USB-hub, buck) whose INTERNAL wiring is fixed and can be copied
verbatim; only the boundary nets (ports) get rebound to the host design and RefDes get
reallocated.
Three-tier library model:
standard-parts.json    → 器件级 (which part / LCSC C-number)
references/blocks/*.json → 电路块级 (how those parts connect + layout constraints)  ← HERE
design-flow.md         → 流程级 (S0–S6 / P0–P10)
FILE LAYOUT — one block per file (references/blocks/<id-without-block-prefix>.json).
The filename is the block id minus the `block.` prefix (ch340c_usb_serial.json →
block.ch340c_usb_serial). This file (`_schema.json`, underscore-prefixed) holds the
shared _doc/_schema/libraryUuid and is NOT a block. `scripts/blocks.py` globs this dir,
skips `_`-prefixed files, and assembles the library — it is the single loader seam, so
consumers (agent, sch block apply) never care that the library is many files. Why per
block: the community model is one-block-per-PR, so a file boundary per block means zero
merge conflicts + clean per-file git-blame attribution. `category` stays a FIELD (filter
with `blocks.py ls --category`), never a directory boundary.
HARD RULE — a block only enters the library as READY after ONE full-flow validation
(place → wire → sch check → DRC = 0) on a real project. Until then `validated: null`
(draft). NEVER hand-write a topology from memory — harvest it from a validated oshwhub
board or an official reference design (see oshwhub training loop). A draft block MAY carry
a proposed `internal_nets` sourced from an official ref, but stays draft until proven; use
the string `pending` when the topology itself is not yet settled.
Pin references use FUNCTIONAL PIN NAMES ("CH340.TXD"), never pin numbers — names are
symbol-stable, so reuse needs zero pin-renumbering. VERIFY names with `sch read` at
validation time (guessed names bite: CH340 is D+/D-/DTR#/RTS#, not UDP/UDM/DTR). `parts`
values point back into standard-parts.json by role key so BOM/LCSC stays single-sourced.
COMMUNITY-BUILT & CREDITED — this is an open, co-built asset. Every block carries its
`author` / `contributors` (GitHub @handles, never removed) so a one-time contribution
earns permanent, traceable credit. Contribution standard, PR checklist, split trigger, and
the validation gate live in references/standard-blocks-contributing.md. Blocks are
versioned (`added`/`updated`) and evolve as parts/footprints change.

<id-without-block-prefix>.json.id: block.<id> — MUST match the filename (minus block.) — blocks.py checks this
<id-without-block-prefix>.json.category: usb-serial | power | mcu-support | button | protection | rf | comms
<id-without-block-prefix>.json.author: original contributor — GitHub @handle;署名不删,让贡献可追、有归属
contributors: @handle of anyone who later fixed/updated this block
<id-without-block-prefix>.json.added: version this block first entered the library, e.g. v0.6.0
<id-without-block-prefix>.json.updated: version of the last change (bump when parts/topology/rules change)
<id-without-block-prefix>.json.validated: null until proven, else 'project@date by @handle: place→wire→check→DRC=0 + netlist proof'

<ROLE>.value_override: optional — override the part's default value for this role
<ROLE>.note: 选型注意 / why this part / substitution caveat

<PORTNAME>.default_net: suggested net name if host has no preference

schematic_notes: 原理图链接注意 — netflag-needs-real-wire, decoupling adjacency in-sheet, net-label
conventions, pins that MUST be tied/pulled, do-not-float pins, etc.

pcb_layout.rule: short slug e.g. usb-diff-pair | decap-adjacency | keepout | trace-width
pcb_layout.value: quantified spec if any (90ohm / <=2mm / >=0.5mm width / etc)

<id-without-block-prefix>.json.bom_note: quantity/cost note across the block (e.g. all 0402, 1 extended part)
